steps.title: Spot the researchers' conclusion
steps.content: The researchers conclude that the prevalence of deceptive displays is mediated by absolute latitude — meaning the percentage of species that use displays varies depending on the latitude of brooding sites.

steps.title: Match that conclusion with the evidence
steps.content:
Choice D shows that 34% of species brooding at 0–30° use displays while 60% at 50–80° do, directly measuring how prevalence changes with latitude across the orders studied.

The other choices either provide insufficient direct evidence (A) or show the relationship depends on order (B and C), contradicting the conclusion.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
